title: [ipv6_addr.c:186-191] BUILD_ASSERT vs _Static_assert conditional compilation
description:
Gap found: Cross-platform assertion pattern not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Preprocessor patterns for portability not examined.

The actual bug: Lines 186-191 use BUILD_ASSERT for Zephyr and _Static_assert for non-Zephyr (host tests). Both check LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN >= 40.

Potential issue: The _Static_assert path (line 189-190) is C11. If compiled with an older C standard (C99), this would fail to compile. The comment doesn't indicate C11 requirement.

The actual check itself is redundant with line 97 '#define LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN 40' - we're asserting >= 40 when the value IS 40. This is defensive but could be simplified to 'LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN == 40' to catch both increases and decreases.

Priority 4 - defensive programming, current code correct for C11+ builds.
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced _Static_assert (C11-only) with portable compile-time assertion using negative-sized array typedef that works with C99 and later. Added comment explaining the portability rationale.
